articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Colombia score first ever win over Spain in London friendly
article.description: Daniel Muñoz scored with an acrobatic kick in the second half to give Colombia a 1-0 win in a friendly against Spain on Friday, their first win ever against the 2010 World Cup champions.
article.linkText: Muñoz gives Colombia 1st ever win over Spain

images.name: Colombia celeb vs. Spain_20240322 [1296x729]
images.alt: Daniel Munoz of Colombia celebrates scoring his team's first goal with teammates during the international friendly match between Spain and Colombia at London Stadium on March 22, 2024 in London, England.

article.story:
Daniel Muñoz scored with an acrobatic kick in the second half to give Colombia a 1-0 win in a friendly against Spain on Friday, their first win ever against the 2010 World Cup champions.

After a dour first half in which both managers rested several key players, Colombia came back with more intensity following the interval, with former Real Madrid attacking midfielder James Rodríguez coming off the bench to lead his side to their fifth consecutive win and extend their unbeaten run to 18 games.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

James and jet-heeled Luis Díaz were a constant menace to a Spanish defence, with Athletic Club centre-back Dani Vivian, who was making his debut with the national team, struggling to stop the talented Liverpool winger.

Díaz was unstoppable motoring up and down their left wing and created several chances that his team mates wasted, two of them to James which first misfired a curling strike way up the bar and later forced keeper Álex Remiro to make a brilliant one-handed save to deny Colombia the opener.

It was not until the 61st minute that they finally managed to score, with James delivering a long pass that Díaz got in stride before slicing Vivian with a fine individual play in the left channel and deliver a cross to Crystal Palace defender Munoz, who netted an acrobatic volley into the top corner.

Colombia kept controlling the proceeding and wasted several other chances to extend their lead against a highly changed Spain, who were left with their back against the wall.

It was Spain's first loss in one year, as manager Luis de la Fuente rested Rodri, Dani Carvajal, Álvaro Morata and several other key players in the build-up to the European Championship in Germany, which starts in June.

He handed international debuts to three players including Vivian and 17-year-old Barcelona defender Pau Cubarsí, who came off the bench late in the second-half.

"We wanted them to live this experience. They are important players with a great future. They still have a long career ahead of them," De La Fuente told Spanish channel TVE. "We learn more from defeats than from victories. We are satisfied with a lot of things. We wanted to test ourselves and continue to improve."

Substitution, Spain. Pau Cubarsí replaces Aymeric Laporte.
